title: 'Future of youth priority': PM Modi says NEET leak national issue, avoid 'partisan politics'
content: More than 170 people, including CJP protesters and Delhi Police personnel, were treated at central government hospitals on Monday following clashes during the 'Chalo Sansad' march, with a young woman admitted to Safdarjung Hospital in critical condition, sources said. According to hospital sources, around 150 injured people were taken to Dr Ram Manohar Lohia (RML) Hospital, while at least 24 others received treatment at Lady Hardinge Medical College (LHMC).

title: India summons Russian diplomat over 4 Indians killed in strike off Ukranian coast
content: Four Indian nationals were killed after a commercial vessel, MV Golden Leo, came under attack while departing Ukraine's port of Odesa on Sunday, the Indian government confirmed. One more Indian crew member remains in critical condition.  According to the government, the vessel had 17 crew members on board, including five Indian nationals, when it was attacked.

title: India Test squad for Lanka tour likely this week; selectors sweat over Sundar
content: India's selectors are set to pick the squad for the two-Test tour of Sri Lanka, with Washington Sundar's hamstring injury the biggest selection concern. Jasprit Bumrah and Ravindra Jadeja are expected to return, while decisions on Sundar's replacement and the all-rounder balance remain pending. India need a strong series to boost their World Test Championship qualification hopes.

title: 'Lokan di avaaz rab di avaaz': Diljit Dosanjh backs CJP protesters
content: Singer Diljit Dosanjh supported student protesters after a police crackdown in Delhi.  He urged authorities to listen to the students' demands and concerns.  The Cockroach Janta Party accused police of excessive force during their march.  Activist Sonam Wangchuk continues his hunger strike after witnessing the events.  Protests at Jantar Mantar persisted despite overnight rain and challenging conditions.

title: Jeff Bezos on returning to work after retirement: I just couldn’t sit on sidelines
content: Jeff Bezos has returned to an operational role to launch his new AI startup, Prometheus.  He describes this demanding work as rewarding, which he calls "Type 2 fun."  Bezos believes artificial intelligence will create a labor shortage, not job losses.  His new venture has secured significant funding and is now his primary focus.  He is energized by the long road ahead as Prometheus scales its operations.

title: Tripura DGP who had led Nirav, anti-Sikh riots probes found dead in office toilet
content: Tripura DGP Anurag Dhankar was found dead in his office toilet on Monday.  A magisterial probe has been ordered into the circumstances surrounding his death.  The government declared one day of state mourning for the respected officer.  Additional DGP Goduguluri Srinivasa Rao has been appointed as the acting police chief.  Investigators are examining all possible factors, including a potential suicide note.

title: Shades of Maradona's 1990 final in Lionel Messi's 2026 journey
content: Two geniuses, two completely different characters, yet so similar in their endeavour to defend the ultimate glory their professional lives had to offer. Messi could easily have walked away after the Qatar high. Not many would have questioned Maradona either if Argentina didn’t match the dizzying heights of 1986 four years on.

title: US judge to Meta's laid off employees: Cannot stop the company from job cuts
content: A US judge has allowed Meta Platforms to proceed with planned layoffs of 26 employees. These employees had sued the company alleging AI-driven discrimination in job cuts. The judge declined an emergency request to halt the layoffs while claims proceed. He stated the court may reconsider if new evidence emerges about AI usage. Meta denies wrongdoing and states human decisions guided the workforce reduction.

title: Pesticides found in 80% seed samples eaten by endangered Australian cockatoos
content: The peril faced by Carnaby's black cockatoos is escalating, as pesticide contamination in their food reaches alarming rates. Testing of seed samples revealed that toxic chemicals present are hundreds of times higher than what is legally permissible. This exposure might shed light on the troubling paralysis syndrome afflicting these endangered birds. Furthermore, researchers identified neurotoxic substances banned in various regions of Europe.

title: Sea anemone can tell helpful bacteria from harmful ones, scientists find
content: Sea anemones distinguish helpful bacteria from harmful invaders without antibodies. Specialized cell structures engulf and destroy foreign bacteria while preserving beneficial microbes. Gene editing revealed a key gene regulating this selective bacterial identification process. This discovery challenges long-held assumptions about early animal immune system evolution. These primitive pathways offer insights into ancient immune memory and maternal transfer.

title: JPMorgan made 40% AI job cuts in some teams, but CEO is not ‘convinced’ of its results
content: JPMorgan Chase has reduced some jobs by up to forty percent using artificial intelligence.  CEO Jamie Dimon believes AI offers no unique competitive advantage for the bank.  He stated that AI will not significantly increase the bank's profit margins.  The bank is increasing recruitment for AI-related roles while hiring fewer bankers.  JPMorgan Chase reported strong net income and investment banking fees for the second quarter.

title: 'Police attacked truck carrying Shabana, Prakash': CJP claims Gijanjali's hair was pulled
content: The Cockroach Janta Party alleges police brutality during a recent Parliament march.  Protesters claim public figures were assaulted and dragged from a truck.  One activist's wife reportedly had her hair pulled during the police action.  The group states their volunteer suffered shattered ribs and other injuries.  They demand the resignation of Dharmendra Pradhan and government dialogue.

title: Shambhu border sealed as farmers head to Delhi to oppose India-US trade deal
content: Farmers marched towards Delhi to protest the proposed Indo-US trade agreement.  Police barricaded the Shambhu border, preventing many from reaching their destination.  Protesters voiced concerns about potential impacts on Indian agriculture and dairy sectors.  They demanded transparency and consultations regarding the trade deal.  The Desh Bachao Morcha called for the government to make agreement details public.

title: Bank refunded insurance premium after borrower's death, denied claim; family gets relief
content: A consumer commission ruled against Jammu and Kashmir Bank and PNB MetLife India Insurance. The bank reversed a loan insurance premium after the borrower's death. This action denied benefits to the deceased's family, which was deemed unfair. The commission ordered the bank and insurer to pay the insurance amount. They were also directed to compensate the family for mental agony and costs.

title: ‘Avengers: Doomsday’ trailer faces VFX backlash; Indian fans prefer ‘Ramayana’
content: The Avengers: Doomsday trailer has generated significant online discussion regarding its visual effects. Many viewers have criticized the CGI as unfinished and unpolished, while others believe it will improve. Indian fans have drawn comparisons to the Ramayana trailer, praising its visual quality. Despite the visual effects debate, demand for Avengers: Doomsday remains exceptionally high. Advance ticket sales for the film have already surpassed other major releases.

title: Can a housing society recover the previous owner's dues from a new buyer? Explained
content: It's essential for buyers to check for outstanding maintenance dues linked to a flat before making a purchase. Apartment societies have the right to claim any unpaid charges from new owners. Local laws and society regulations govern who is responsible for these dues. Under certain agreements, buyers can recover any paid amounts from the previous owner, so securing a no-dues statement in writing is vital before closing the deal.

title: MP grandmother held for killing 20-day-old baby girl over desire for boy
content: A 20-day-old baby girl was allegedly strangled by her grandmother in Tikamgarh. The 60-year-old accused reportedly desired a male grandchild, police stated. She allegedly took the infant outside and committed the act while the mother slept. The grandmother then attempted to conceal the death as natural. Police arrested the accused, who is now in judicial custody.

title: Massive blaze at Delhi residential building triggers cylinder explosions; 8 rescued
content: A large fire erupted in a Delhi residential building's parking area. Two cylinders exploded, and four vehicles sustained damage in the blaze. Firefighters safely rescued eight individuals trapped within the building. The incident occurred in the Hari Nagar locality on Tuesday morning. The exact cause of the fire remains under investigation.

title: How human-bred fish that escaped into Philippines crater lake is biodiversity risk
content: Flowerhorn cichlids, bred by humans, have found their way into Lake Sampaloc, a crater lake in the Philippines. Their presence poses a significant threat to the local ecosystem, competing aggressively with native ayungin fish and disrupting aquaculture. Experts raise alarms about the potential for disease transmission and the rapid expansion of this invasive species, underscoring a pressing global issue of domesticated animals affecting natural habitats.

title: Scientists marked this coral reef as dead in 1960s; drones found it alive...
content: After being presumed extinct for sixty years, a remarkable coral reef off West Africa has been rediscovered, thriving with an array of fish and coral species. This vibrant mesophotic coral ecosystem thrives in low-light environments beneath the ocean's surface. Researchers speculate that similar hidden reefs could exist along the West African coastline, providing valuable insights into the evolutionary history of the region's marine life and past environmental changes.

title: 'Pradhan has to be sacked': Sonam Wangchuk enters Day 24 of hunger strike
content: Sonam Wangchuk continues his 24th day hunger strike demanding accountability for the NEET paper leak.  Protesters resumed their sit-in at Jantar Mantar, pressing for the education minister's resignation.  A recent march to Parliament drew significant support from various personalities and MPs.  Allegations of police force against protesters were made by the CJP group.  However, Delhi Police rejected these claims as false and misleading information.

title: Kerala merchant navy man dies in Russian strike off Ukraine ahead of wedding
content: A 26-year-old merchant navy officer from Kerala died after his ship was hit. The cargo vessel was reportedly struck by Russian forces near Odesa. Akhil Joyan was due to marry in two months and was the sole provider. His family is seeking assistance to bring his body home. India condemned the attack on commercial shipping and civilian lives.

title: Argentina players could face FIFA bans after World Cup 2026 final chaos against Spain
content: FIFA has launched an investigation into post-match clashes following Argentina's World Cup final loss. Several Argentina players were involved in confrontations with Spanish counterparts after the game. FIFA's Disciplinary Committee has appointed a prosecutor to examine these potential breaches. Players found guilty of violent conduct face a minimum three-match suspension. The Argentine Football Association could also face disciplinary action and fines.

title: Himachal schools closed today in 4 districts; IMD red alert prompts precautionary shutdown
content: Himachal Pradesh school holiday: Educational institutions across Kangra, Mandi, Chamba and Sirmaur districts of Himachal Pradesh are closed today, July 21, 2026, following an IMD Red Alert for heavy rainfall, flash floods and landslides. Examinations scheduled in the affected districts have been postponed, while teaching and non-teaching staff have been granted leave, subject to emergency duties. Several other districts remain open, although local authorities have been authorised to suspend classes if weather conditions deteriorate.

title: Top stocks to buy today: Stock market recommendations for July 21, 2026 - check list
content: Stock market recommendations: Bank of India, Computer Age Management Services (CAMS), Bharti Airtel, and PFC - Somil Mehta, Head of Retail Research at Mirae Asset Sharekhan recommends these as the top stocks to buy on July 21, 2026:

title: How Argentina ruined Messi's 'Last Dance'
content: Spain win the World Cup, Argentina win the charge sheet, and Lionel Messi’s final bow turns into football’s dirtiest farewell.

title: PM Modi congratulates Andy Burnham on becoming UK Prime Minister
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi congratulated Andy Burnham on his new role.  He expressed confidence that India-UK ties will deepen across key sectors.  The two nations share democratic values and cooperate widely in many areas.  With CETA entering into force, the partnership is poised for growth.  Modi looks forward to working closely to strengthen the comprehensive strategic partnership.

title: Who will ask if you’re home? The economy growing around urban loneliness
content: India faces a growing loneliness crisis as its population ages rapidly. Urbanization and changing social structures contribute to this increasing isolation. Young migrants and older adults alike experience profound feelings of not belonging. The emerging "silver economy" offers market-based solutions for connection and care. This situation highlights the need to address loneliness as a fundamental human requirement.

title: Fact Check: Did Naseeruddin Shah attend the CJP protest?
content: A video shows Naseeruddin Shah and his sons at Jantar Mantar. They were reportedly present during a student protest which gained social media attention. The actor and his sons observed the demonstration quietly without making any public statements. Social media users shared the footage, noting the actor's silent presence. The video circulated widely, prompting comments about his attendance at the event.

title: 'Rohit aur Virat ko touch nahi kar payenge': Ashwin makes bold statement
content: Ravichandran Ashwin supports Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli's continued selection for India. He cited their extensive career achievements and significant fan support as key factors. Ashwin noted that bowlers do not receive the same level of public backing. Rohit Sharma recently silenced retirement speculation with a century against England. The captain stated that external criticism has always been part of his career.

title: 'The pain is immense': Lionel Messi pens emotional note after Argentina lose World Cup final
content: Spain defeated Argentina in the FIFA World Cup 2026 final after extra time. Lionel Messi shared an emotional message acknowledging the immense pain of the loss. He expressed pride in the team's achievement of reaching consecutive World Cup finals. Substitute Ferran Torres scored the decisive goal in the 106th minute of the match. The defeat ended Argentina's hopes of defending their title successfully.

title: 118 cops & 60 marchers injured: Cops block protesters' march to Parl; Nadda meets CJP leaders
content: A protest by the Cockroach Janta Party demanding a minister's resignation turned chaotic. Protesters clashed with police, resulting in numerous injuries on both sides. Police reported detentions and filed charges related to rioting and assault on public servants. The BJP blamed Left-affiliated student groups for instigating the organized violence. Investigations are underway to establish criminal responsibility for the disturbances.

title: Trump imposes 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods, cites trade disputes
content: President Trump imposed fifty percent tariffs on most Canadian goods. This action escalates trade tensions between the two nations. The tariffs apply to many goods previously protected under the USMCA agreement. This move heightens economic uncertainty and strains bilateral ties. Aides are examining the possibility of imposing additional tariffs on Canada.

title: 'They will pay many times over': Trump as US carries out 10th night of Iran strikes
content: United States forces conducted tenth night of strikes against Iran.  These operations aim to reopen the Strait of Hormuz after recent attacks.  Iran also targeted US allies Kuwait, Jordan, and Bahrain with missile barrages.  Diplomatic efforts continue while the interim ceasefire agreement has collapsed.  Global energy supplies face fresh concerns as oil prices climb.

title: 'Ramayana' trailer reviews: Ranbir starrer hailed as 'SPECTACULAR'
content: Filmmakers and industry insiders have shared overwhelmingly positive early reactions to the 'Ramayana' trailer. They praised the film's grand scale, visual effects, and performances by the cast. Ranbir Kapoor as Lord Rama and Yash as Ravana received specific commendations for their roles. The trailer is set to be unveiled publicly on July 24. This cinematic adaptation of the Indian epic is slated for a Diwali 2026 worldwide release.

title: CJP protest in Delhi LIVE: Dipke apologises to CJP supporters who were 'brutally beaten' by police
content: The police action on one of the largest protests in the national capital in recent years left several people injured

title: Killing of 4 Indian seafarers: India summons Russian envoy
content: Sources said the MEA told the Russian diplomat that the attack on the merchant vessel was unacceptable

title: 5,289 cyber awareness programmes held across Gujarat
content: The campaign focuses on preventing cybercrime through community engagement, technology-driven surveillance, victim outreach, cyber awareness and prompt legal action

title: Karnataka HC dismisses PIL complaining procedural lapse in one notification of acquiring 518 acres for Bidadi township project
content: However, court clarifies that this order is not an expression of opinion on other issues related to acquisition notifications

title: SIR: Voters above 85 years face double checks, say BLOs
content: Booth Level Officers say they have been instructed to place them under the “probable absent, shifted, dead/deleted or duplicate (ASDD)” category, Chief Electoral Officer denies claims.

title: Tunnel collapses in Sikkim’s Namchi; 8 workers killed, rescue ops under way
content: The workers were trapped inside the under-construction tunnel after a landslide blocked its entrance and triggered a suspected gas leak, officials said

title: IED destroyed in controlled blast in J&K's Rajouri
content: The IED was noticed by an army search party in village Argi Dalhori late Monday (July 20, 2026) evening, the officials said

title: Woman-founded Amaravati startup Bringgg secures ₹40 lakh NRI investment
content: Logistics startup Bringgg aims to create a peer-to-peer logistics network that connects travellers, senders and seekers while helping reduce or offset travel costs

title: BJP protest in Belagavi alleging delay in declaring drought in Karnataka
content: Protestors walked from Kittur Channamma Circle to the office of the Deputy Commissioner, and shouted slogans against the government

title: Minister seeks to rope in RWAs in sapling plantation drive ‘Bengaluru Hasirooru’ to be launched on August 15
content: To meet the target, the GBA corporations have collectively earmarked ₹9.64 crore in their budgets, covering both plantation and years of maintenance

title: Krishna Byre Gowda targets ‘paper plantation’ in Bengaluru
content: According to the Minister, the civic body has not been planting trees as per its claims and has been showing inflated numbers on paper owing to a lack of accountability and improper documentation

title: From IAC to paper leaks: Why anti-system movements pose a unique political challenge
content: The IAC movement consciously projected itself as a citizens’ campaign, even though the Congress-led UPA maintained that it represented a collaboration between the RSS and civil society activists

title: Martyrs’ Day 2026 turns out to be show of political strength for rival Trinamool factions in West Bengal
content: Trinamool Congress MP Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar, who, along with 19 other party Lok Sabha MPs, has merged with the little-known Nationalist Citizens Party of India (NCPI), said the faction would observe Martyrs’ Day at Rajghat

title: Green Line services of Namma Metro in Bengaluru disrupted for 45 minutes after power breakdown
content: BMRCL expressed regret for the inconvenience caused, and thanked commuters for their patience and cooperation

title: Leave with all your belongings, Mamata tells deserters
content: Mamata Banerjee, who is facing one of the biggest challenges of her political life, said those who remain with her will be her “greatest strength”, and she will rebuild the Trinamool Congress with them

title: TMC MLA Madan Mitra attends Mamata camp's assembly protest days after joining rebel faction
content: Clarifying his presence at the protest, Madan Mitra said he had remained there purely as a matter of courtesy after emerging from Ritabrata Banerjee's chamber in the Assembly, and insisted that it did not signify any change in his political position.

title: Pothundy double murder case: a death penalty verdict with rare focus on rehabilitating victims’ family
content: Court directs District Legal Services Authority, Palakkad, to initiate rehabilitation measures for orphaned children of the victims; legal experts say such a specific direction was rare in criminal judgments

title: AI in skill education instead of third language in select high schools; BEOs instructed to appoint one ‘AI guest teacher’ per school
content: The Block Education Officer concerned has been instructed to appoint one ‘AI guest teacher’ per school to teach AI

title: Political rivalries clash over Karnataka’s Bidadi township project
content: The Bidadi township project envisages acquiring about 9,600 acres across 25 villages in the Bengaluru South district.

title: UAS-B introduces low-cost technology to farmers to produce biochar, trains them to improve soil health
content: The team from UAS-B has used a simple and portable drum technology that doesn’t cost much to produce biochar

title: Suvendu makes public July 21 police firing report; slams Mamata for not acting on it when she was CM
content: Suvendu Adhikari says the former Chief Minister had always claimed to be the prime witness in the July 21, 1993 firing, but she was not questioned by the commission to give her statement

title: March to Parliament: Protesters’ anger extends beyond paper leaks
content: One of the many common threads connecting those who faced police lathis was that they had voted for the BJP in the past or came from families that had traditionally supported the BJP, making their disappointment more acute

title: The fine art of creating blood donors at home; and resources for those in Chennai seeking to donate blood
content: A leitmotif among blood donation organisations is the increasing difficulty in finding people with “generous veins”; and the solution could strangely be found in the living rooms of households, as illustrated by these two accounts from Chennai of parents inspiring their children to join the cause

title: TVK government in Tamil Nadu faces a fiscal test in balancing populism and prudence
content: “We can have a welfare State only if we work for it and step up production,” said the then Chief Minister C. Rajagopalachari in June 1952

title: Sonam Wangchuk to continue fast, cites police action against protesters
content: Mt. Wangchuk said he is "touched and moved" by the manner in which the protesters have maintained peace despite "provocation"

title: Panchayat, local body elections in Rajasthan to be completed by November 15
content: The Bharatiya Janata Party government submitted the schedule for panchayat and local body elections in the Rajasthan High Court during the hearing on a batch of public interest petitions

title: Gujarat destroys drugs worth ₹2,153 crore at Dahej
content: “The Gujarat Police have dealt a major blow to international drug smuggling networks through decisive action against drug mafias and cartels,” Mr. Sanghavi

title: Six killed in floods, landslips in Northeast
content: Railway authorities cancelled, diverted, or short-terminated several passenger train services in Assam. At least 25 people have been killed across four States since the last week of June

title: Chief Minister Samrat Choudhary flags off special train carrying 1,100 pilgrims to Somnath Dham
content: Bihar government will bear the entire cost of the seven-day pilgrimage. Free pilgrimage will take place from July 20 to 26

title: Assam unveils mission to convert Muga silk waste into high-value yarn
content: Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma says that about 40% of the cocoons yielding the golden silk currently go unused due to the absence of proper processing facilities

title: ED provisionally attached 389 immovable properties worth ₹240.03 Crore
content: ED initiated investigation on the basis of multiple FIRs registered by the Uttar Pradesh Police and the Delhi Police against BIIPL, GVCTPL, Satinder Singh Bhasin and others, under various Sections of the Indian Penal Code, 1860

title: BMC BJP corporator demands regular audit of the birth registration
content: Several corporators highlighted that the delay in birthday certificates is derailing study-abroad plans, visa applications for scores of residents, and document problems

title: More than half of Odisha’s PVTG adult women anaemic, says a study by Utkal University
content: Among PVTGs, the Hill Kharia shows the highest stunting prevalence at 82.76%, a distressing figure that signifies a critical public health emergency, the study raises alarm

title: Aston Villa signs João Gomes as star Morgan Rogers completes British record move to Chelsea
content: Gomes arrives days after club record signing Johan Manzambi, who cost more than 50 million pounds ($67 million) from Freiburg after a stellar World Cup with Switzerland

title: Supreme Court proposes SIT probe into Ram Temple case
content: Bench tells petitioners not to play politics with the donation embezzlement case; complainants had sought a CBI probe and publication of all financial details of Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust; court lists case for orders on July 27

title: 7 killed in landslide in Jammu and Kashmir as rain toll hits 21
content: The J&K Police actively participated in the rescue operation at Domail Dhok after a tragic landslide hit a mud house, claiming seven lives; rescue efforts are continuing in coordination with all agencies concerned, said the police

title: Monsoon Session off to a stormy start amid Opposition protests
content: Opposition MPs raise NEET paper leak, Ram Temple donation theft issues; Lok Sabha adjourned after repeated disruptions; MPs of Congress, SP and Trinamool troop into the Well of the House; DMK raises Mekedatu issue, skips Congress-led meeting

title: Several hurt in crackdown on CJP Parliament march
content: Scores hospitalised with fractures, head and eye injuries; CJP leaders meet Nadda, demand Pradhan’s ouster; Wangchuk cites ‘brutality’, says will continue fast till MPs meet ‘youth leaders’ or him

title: Police outnumbered, taken aback by number of protesters who reached Parliament’s doorstep
content: In an unprecedented turn of events, despite multiple layers of barricades, deployment of around 3,000 security personnel and an Internet shutdown, large crowds comprising mostly students and young people were able to reach the doorsteps of Parliament before they were tear-gassed and charged with batons

title: Structural stability assessment of KWA reservoirs in Kochi begins
content: The French firm SUEZ has begun non-destructive testing of the water utility’s reservoirs, pump houses and other infrastructure

title: Former CorroHealth staff say compensation promise is a relief
content: Government-mediated talks resulted in the company agreeing to pay five months’ gross salary as additional compensation for laid-off employees, says Minister; compensation to be paid on July 26

title: Defections affect moral sanctity of democracy
content: The greatest victim of political defections is not the Opposition party that loses a legislator. It is the voter

title: Indus Waters Treaty will not remain operational in its present form, says official
content: He says India has placed the treaty in “abeyance” after the Pahalgam terror attack and ever since there has been no change in Pakistan’s sponsorship of terrorists

title: Stock Market LIVE Updates, Sensex Today: Sensex Sheds 250 Points Flat Amid US-Iran Tensions
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Crude oil prices retreat from 1-month high amid US-Iran war tensions; Brent at $88 per barrel.

title: Pune Man Kills Father's Murder Accused 23 Years After He Was Killed
content: A grudge born in infancy turned deadly, as Vaidya and three accomplices allegedly tracked down Kadu-Deshmukh near Pune's Katraj tunnel.

title: FBI Issues 'Wanted' Notice Against Indian National In Drug Trafficking Case
content: The crackdown has already netted 24 arrests and 37 indictments across the US, Canada and Europe for smuggling cocaine and methamphetamine.

title: Indian Man Jailed For Attempted Theft Of Diamond Worth $1,54,000 In Singapore
content: Indian national Mangroliya Manojkumar Kurjibhai jailed in Singapore after swapping a genuine diamond worth USD154,000 with a fake at a Chinatown store

title: US-Iran War Highlights: US Conducts Strikes On Iran For 9th Straight Night After Another Soldier Killed
content: US-Iran War News Highlights: CENTCOM said the raids were designed to "swiftly punish Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps forces who launched attacks against American service members in Jordan last night."

title: 27 Dead, 83 Missing As Ferry Overturns Off Guyana Coast
content: At least 27 people are dead and 83 more are missing after a ferry capsized off the coast of Guyana over the weekend, Prime Minister Mark Phillips said Monday.

title: Wes Streeting Named New UK Defence Minister
content: Britain's new Prime Minister Andy Burnham named ex-health minister Wes Streeting as his defence secretary late Monday, the government said, as ministerial appointments ran into the night.

title: 'Engaged Weeks Ago, Wanted New Job': Kerala Sailor Killed In Russian Attack
content: Joyan's family got to know about his death through an email received on Monday evening from the shipping company he worked with.

title: 7 killed, Several Injured As Bus Bursts Into Flames In Vietnam
content: The bus was traveling from Lam Dong province to the nearby business hub Ho Chi Minh City when at caught fire.

title: Political Row Erupts In Bihar Over Saffron Colour Plan For 500 New Model Schools
content: Bihar Chief Minister Samrat Choudhary, who inaugurated the schools in Begusarai on Sunday, launched the initiative that involves repainting the buildings in a shade identified by the education department as "Sunrise" (colour code 0526), with doors painted gold brown.

title: Stock Market Highlights, Sensex Today: Sensex Closes 440 Points Lower As Oil Tops $90 Amid US-Iran War
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty Highlights: Oil prices jump 3% as US-Iran conflict intensifies, Brent crude tops $90 per barrel.

title: Punjab Farmers To Take Part In Rally Against India-US Trade Deal Today
content: Farmers from Punjab, Haryana, Himachal Pradesh and Rajasthan will gather at Kisan Ghat, Delhi, to oppose the proposed India-US trade deal.

title: Video: Vijay Conducts Surprise Inspection Of Chennai's Bio-CNG Plant
content: Nearly half of Chennai's 6,150-tonne daily waste is non-biodegradable, prompting Chief Minister Vijay's push for a statewide scientific fix.

title: CJP Parliament March LIVE Updates: "Protests To Continue," Says CJP After Meeting With JP Nadda
content: CJP Parliament March LIVE Updates: Union minister JP Nadda and CJP members Saurav Das and Ashutosh Ranka met in Delhi on Monday.

title: Son Of Brazil's Ex-President Bolsonaro Secures US Green Card
content: Eduardo Bolsonaro said he moved to protect himself from what he called unjust persecution by Brazil's Supreme Court, not help his father.

title: 8 Killed, Several Trapped In Sikkim Tunnel After Landslide Triggers Gas Leak
content: Officials said methane levels inside the tunnel remained high initially that rescuers were unable to reach the location

title: "Discriminatory Treatment": US Imposes New 50% Tariffs On Certain Canadian Goods
content: US President Donald Trump signed orders Monday to impose new 50-percent tariffs on some Canadian goods, citing "discriminatory treatment" in the areas of alcohol, automobiles and dairy products.

title: France Says Two Of Its Diplomats "Detained, Physically Abused" In Iran
content: Two French diplomats in Iran suffered "an extremely serious act of intimidation" by Islamic republic security services, with one of them physically abused, France's foreign minister said Monday.

title: Monsoon Car Damage? Here's When Insurance Pays And When It Won't
content: Instead of attempting repairs or restarting the engine, owners should immediately inform their insurer, click photographs or record videos of damage.

title: Two premier hospital RDAs seek President's intervention over Wangchuk case
content: Anuja Jaiswal is a Senior Assistant Editor at The Times of India, with an impressive 18-year career in narrative journalism. She specializes in health and heritage reporting, expertly simplifying complex health information to make it engaging and understandable for readers. Her deep dives into heritage topics are well-researched, resulting in captivating narratives that resonate with her audience. Over the years, she has worked in Chandigarh, Chhattisgarh and West UP, gaining diverse on-ground experience that shapes her storytelling. Read More

content: the indus waters treaty with Pakistan will no longer function in its present form . a source said it's not in India's interest to remain a party to the treaty anymore . the government has concluded hardening its position on the contentious water issue .

title: Four Indians killed in Russian strike on vessel off Ukrainian port
content: four Indian nationals were reported to have died in an attack on a commercial vessel . at the time of the incident, there were 17 crew members on board, including 5 Indians . india condemned the attack, reaffirming that targeting commercial shipping is deplorable and must be avoided .
